title: Management Accounting Mock Mid Exam 1 – Costing and Cost Systems
description: Assesses analytical and applied understanding of management accounting concepts, cost behavior, cost assignment, and job and process costing.
subject: ACC2370 - Management Accounting

questions.question: Which situation best justifies the use of activity based costing instead of a single plant wide rate?
options: Overheads form a small proportion of total cost and products are homogeneous
Direct labour hours strongly correlate with all overhead activities
Products consume support activities in significantly different proportions
Production volume is the only significant cost driver
questions.answer: Products consume support activities in significantly different proportions
questions.rationale: ABC is most beneficial when overheads are driven by diverse activities and products consume those activities unevenly. Using a single volume based rate would distort costs.

questions.question: In an activity based costing system, which of the following best describes an activity cost driver?
options: A measure that directly traces materials to finished goods
A factor that causes a change in the cost of an activity
A budgeted overhead absorption rate per unit
A financial account used to record indirect costs
questions.answer: A factor that causes a change in the cost of an activity
questions.rationale: Cost drivers explain why activity costs are incurred. Confusing them with allocation rates or accounts leads to incorrect cost assignment.

questions.question: A company absorbs overheads using a rate based on machine hours. Which implicit assumption is being made?
options: Machine hours vary directly with all overhead costs
Machine hours are fixed regardless of output level
Labour time is insignificant in production
Overheads are entirely variable with units produced
questions.answer: Machine hours vary directly with all overhead costs
questions.rationale: Volume based absorption assumes a strong cause and effect relationship between the chosen base and overheads. Ignoring this assumption leads to miscosting.

questions.question: Which cost is most likely to be treated as a step fixed cost?
options: Salary of a production supervisor hired per shift
questions.answer: Salary of a production supervisor hired per shift
questions.rationale: Step fixed costs remain constant over a range but increase when capacity thresholds are crossed. Treating them as variable is a common error.

questions.question: Which of the following costs would be classified as a period cost?
questions.answer: Administrative office rent
questions.rationale: Period costs are expensed in the period incurred and are not attached to inventory. Mixing them with product costs overstates inventory values.

questions.question: In job costing, which document primarily accumulates costs for a specific job?
questions.answer: Job cost sheet
questions.rationale: The job cost sheet summarizes all direct and absorbed costs for a job. Confusing source documents with summary records is a common pitfall.

questions.question: If overheads are absorbed as a percentage of prime cost, which cost will directly affect the overhead absorbed?
questions.answer: Direct material
questions.rationale: Prime cost consists of direct material and direct labour. Overhead linked to prime cost fluctuates with these components, not indirect items.

questions.question: Which statement correctly distinguishes process costing from job costing?
options: Process costing accumulates costs by job while job costing accumulates by department
Process costing is suitable for homogeneous continuous production
Job costing ignores work in progress valuation
Process costing does not require cost allocation
questions.answer: Process costing is suitable for homogeneous continuous production
questions.rationale: Process costing applies where identical units pass through continuous processes. Misapplying job costing here leads to unnecessary complexity.

questions.question: Which factor would most likely reduce the accuracy of a plant wide overhead rate?
options: Multiple products with diverse support requirements
questions.answer: Multiple products with diverse support requirements
questions.rationale: Diverse products require different activities. A single rate masks this diversity and causes cross subsidization.

questions.question: A cost that has already been incurred and cannot be changed by future decisions is known as:
questions.answer: Sunk cost
questions.rationale: Sunk costs are irrelevant for decision making. Including them leads to biased and irrational decisions.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why returned direct materials are deducted from job cost?
options: They are no longer consumed by the job
They convert direct costs into indirect costs
questions.answer: They are no longer consumed by the job
questions.rationale: Only materials actually used should be charged to a job. Ignoring returns overstates job costs.

questions.question: In process costing, equivalent units are primarily used to:
options: Measure partially completed production in terms of full units
questions.answer: Measure partially completed production in terms of full units
questions.rationale: Equivalent units convert incomplete work into comparable complete units. Confusing them with pricing or loss treatment is incorrect.

questions.question: Which overhead allocation base is least appropriate for allocating setup costs?
questions.answer: Machine hours
questions.rationale: Setup costs are batch related, not volume related. Using machine hours spreads setup costs inaccurately across units.

questions.question: A company experiences under absorption of overheads. Which situation is most likely?
options: Actual overhead incurred was higher than absorbed
Production volume was higher than planned
questions.answer: Actual overhead incurred was higher than absorbed
questions.rationale: Under absorption arises when absorbed overhead is less than actual overhead. Confusing volume effects with cost effects is common.

questions.question: Which cost classification is most useful for short term decision making?
questions.answer: Relevant costs
questions.rationale: Relevant costs differ between alternatives and affect decisions. Including sunk or historical costs clouds judgment.

questions.question: If total setup cost is Rs 250000 and there are 100 production runs, what is the setup cost per run?
questions.rationale: Dividing total cost by total cost driver units gives the rate. Errors often arise from confusing units and batches.

questions.question: Which statement about cost estimation using high low method is correct?
options: It uses all available data points
It separates fixed and variable elements using extreme activity levels
It provides the most statistically accurate estimate
questions.answer: It separates fixed and variable elements using extreme activity levels
questions.rationale: High low relies only on highest and lowest points. Overreliance without checking outliers can mislead.

questions.question: Which cost would normally be excluded from prime cost?
questions.answer: Indirect labour
questions.rationale: Prime cost includes only direct material and direct labour. Including indirect labour inflates prime cost incorrectly.

questions.question: In an ABC system, what is the primary reason for allocating resource costs to activity cost pools?
options: To improve tracing of overheads to products
questions.answer: To improve tracing of overheads to products
questions.rationale: Activities act as an intermediate stage to create a clearer cause and effect link. Cost reduction is not automatic.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key weakness of job costing?
options: High record keeping and administrative effort
questions.answer: High record keeping and administrative effort
questions.rationale: Tracking costs job by job increases accuracy but also complexity. Ignoring this trade off is risky.

questions.question: Which scenario most likely results in cost distortion under traditional costing?
questions.answer: High automation with multiple products
questions.rationale: Automation increases overhead significance and diversity, making traditional volume based costing misleading.

questions.question: In job costing, [dropdown_1] is used to record labour time, while [dropdown_2] summarizes total job costs.
questions.rationale: Time sheets capture labour input and job cost sheets accumulate all job related costs.

questions.question: A [dropdown_1] cost changes in total with activity, while a [dropdown_2] cost remains constant within a relevant range.
questions.rationale: Understanding cost behavior is essential for budgeting and forecasting.

questions.question: Process costing is most appropriate when products are [dropdown_1] and production is [dropdown_2].
questions.rationale: Process costing suits continuous flows of identical units.

questions.question: The cost that represents the benefit foregone by choosing one alternative over another is called ________ cost.
questions.answer: opportunity
questions.rationale: Opportunity cost reflects sacrificed benefits and is crucial in decision making.

questions.question: Costs that can be directly traced to a cost object without allocation are known as ________ costs.
questions.answer: direct
questions.rationale: Direct costs have a clear traceable relationship to the cost object.

questions.question: In process costing, partially completed units are converted into ________ units for costing purposes.
questions.answer: equivalent
questions.rationale: Equivalent units allow comparison between complete and incomplete output.

questions.question: Which of the following are characteristics of activity based costing?
questions.rationale: ABC uses several pools and drivers to better reflect resource consumption. A single driver contradicts ABC logic.

questions.question: Which costs are included in prime cost?
questions.rationale: Prime cost includes only direct material and direct labour.

questions.question: Which situations make job costing more appropriate than process costing?
questions.rationale: Job costing suits unique, order specific production rather than continuous identical output.

questions.question: Match each cost type with its correct description.

matchPairs.right: Already incurred and not relevant for decisions
Benefit foregone from the next best alternative
Changes in total with activity level

questions.question: Match the document to its primary purpose in job costing.

matchPairs.right: Authorizes issue of materials to a job
Records labour hours spent on jobs
Summarizes total cost of a job

questions.question: Match each costing method with the most suitable production environment.
Explain why traditional volume based overhead absorption can lead to product cost distortion in modern manufacturing environments.
Discuss how job costing information can support managerial planning and control decisions.
